methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 18 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Core Automation, a stealth-stage AI & Software company founded by Gerald Depew and Daniel C. Rozok, is targeting a Series funding round of $500M–$1B at a $4–5B+ post-money valuation. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers including Palantir, Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Applied Intuition—a total of nine data points across the AI & Software sector.

Standard peer-median triangulation is not applicable here. The target's stealth stage and valuation tier do not overlap sufficiently with same-stage or adjacent-stage peers in the broader AI & Software sector. While 18 same-sector comparables exist, none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage alignment, comparable valuation tier, and minimum dataset size (≥3 datapoints). Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial selection bias and mask meaningful differences in business model maturity, revenue traction, and market positioning.

Without sufficient narrow-peer data to anchor a reliable multiple-based approach, the stated target range of $4–5B+ remains the primary valuation anchor. Key uncertainties include the actual revenue base and growth trajectory (not disclosed in stealth status), the capital intensity and burn profile relative to named peers, and whether the company's technology defensibility and market timing align with frontier-stage precedent. A formal valuation would require disclosure of financial performance and a refined comparable set filtered by stage and operational similarity.
